verification_note: Model IDs and pricing verified 2026-05-15. /model-audit re-verifies monthly. Verify live before any cost-sensitive operation.

codex.invocation: local Codex CLI — `codex exec` (general) / `codex exec review` (code review)
codex.underlying_model: gpt-5.5 (OpenAI GPT-5.5 family, accessed via Codex)
codex.pricing_note: the maintainered via the Codex CLI account, not the stack's API metering. Codex calls are not metered into cost_log.
codex.notes: v1.1.1 adaptation: the stack reaches the OpenAI / GPT-5.5 family through the locally-installed, authenticated Codex CLI rather than a direct OpenAI API key. Claude Code cannot run subagents on non-Claude models, so adversarial roles (reviewer, product-critic, security-auditor) run on a Claude model and orchestrate Codex via bash. Replaces the artifacts' `openai` provider block. See ADR-011. ADR-028: macOS Keychain item 'openai-api-key' (env OPENAI_API_KEY wins) is a resilient BACKUP for the direct-API rung — scripts/lib/openai-key.sh `oai_export` fills OPENAI_API_KEY from it when unset, so the OpenAI family survives a Codex CLI auth loss (the Gemini-CLI-death lesson, ADR-027).

gemini.invocation: Gemini REST API via scripts/lib/gemini-api.sh (`gmn_call`); the CLI is DEAD (IneligibleTierError, 2026-06-30)
gemini.pricing_note: billed via the Gemini API key; like codex/deepseek it is NOT metered into cost_log.
gemini.notes: ADR-012 REVISED 2026-06-30: the Gemini CLI free tier returns IneligibleTierError ('client no longer supported … migrate to Antigravity'), so the CLI path is gone. The Gemini family is now reached via the REST API (scripts/lib/gemini-api.sh), pinned to gemini-3.1-pro-preview — this also resolves the /model-audit recommendation to stop the CLI auto-downgrade to 3.5 Flash. The API has NO filesystem access, so the orchestrating agent assembles repo context and pipes it in (unlike the old CLI). Gemini roles: architecture-critic, red-team, historian. Cross-family (ADR-011): non-Claude — never point at a Claude model. 'preview' model id — revisit at /model-audit.

deepseek-cn.data_residency: CHINA — api.deepseek.com is hosted in China. ADR-029: routine/non-sensitive diffs ONLY; the helper hard-blocks high-stakes/sensitive content (auth/crypto/secret/payment/migration, sensitivity=high, domain-mode security|schema-migration).
deepseek-cn.pricing_note: billed via the DeepSeek API key; like codex/gemini it is NOT metered into cost_log.
deepseek-cn.notes: ADR-026 + ADR-029: DeepSeek-v4 third adversarial voice via scripts/lib/deepseek-review.sh, advisory. CHINA-HOSTED → ROUTINE/non-sensitive diffs ONLY; the helper's data-residency guard hard-blocks (return 8) any high-stakes/sensitive content regardless of caller. Wired into reviewer (routine), effectively-off for security-auditor, and NOT used by red-team (always high-stakes). Cross-family (ADR-011): non-Claude — never point at a Claude model. deepseek-chat/reasoner ids retire 2026-07-24; use v4 ids. Key: macOS Keychain 'deepseek-cn-api-key' (env DEEPSEEK_CN_API_KEY).

reviewer.notes: v1.1.1: adversarial review via local Codex CLI — see ADR-011. ADR-025: tiered by stakes via scripts/lib/review-router.sh — high-stakes diffs use codex/gpt-5.5@high; routine diffs use local Qwen first, escalate to codex/gpt-5.4. Both tiers stay cross-family (never Claude). Model IDs resolved from review_tiers below.

product-critic.notes: v1.1.1: product critique via local Codex CLI — see ADR-011. ADR-025: tiered by stakes (review-router.sh); product critique is rarely high-stakes so usually routes routine (local Qwen). Cross-family preserved.

red-team.notes: v1.1.1: red-team analysis via local Gemini CLI — see ADR-012.

security-auditor.notes: v1.1.1: primary audit via local Codex CLI; belt-and-suspenders Opus second pass on novel crypto/auth — see ADR-011. ADR-025: tiered by stakes (review-router.sh). Security work classifies high more often (security/auth/crypto paths → gpt-5.5@high); the Opus second pass on novel crypto/auth is UNCHANGED.

architecture-critic.notes: v1.1.1: whole-repo architectural critique via local Gemini CLI — see ADR-012.

historian.notes: v1.1.1: long-context history review via local Gemini CLI — see ADR-012. Tier 4 subagent.

foreman-team-lead.notes: v1.1: Used in agent-teams orchestration mode only. Main-thread mode uses foreman as a skill — no subagent invocation.

local-ops.notes: Tier 5 local subagent. Model string must match an ollama tag that tier-installer.sh actually pulls and HARDWARE.md recommends — keep these three in sync.

model_fit._doc: ADR-033 post-session receipt: the Anthropic tier ladder the receipt walks (cheap->strong). Ids MUST exist in providers.anthropic.models. /model-audit keeps this fresh alongside pricing. Ratio bands live in the hook/lib, not here.

review_tiers._doc: ADR-025 adversarial-review router tiers. Read by scripts/lib/review-router.sh (env var > this block > built-in default). Repoint models here when the Tier-5 local box lands — no script edit. Every tier MUST be a non-Claude family (ADR-011 cross-family rule); never set a Claude model here.
review_tiers._doc_codex_transport: ADR-030: HOW the OpenAI/GPT-5.5 family is reached is a separate setting `codex_transport` (api|cli), NOT configured here. Default 'api' (scripts/lib/openai-review.sh, oair_call → OpenAI API); 'cli' uses `codex exec` with automatic API fallback. Resolution (highest first): env REVIEW_CODEX_TRANSPORT > project .claude/stack-config.json .review.codex_transport > default 'api'. Set per-project via /project-init, per-session via /session.

high.when: diff touches auth/crypto/payment/migration/RLS/secret paths, or domain-mode security|schema-migration, or sensitivity=high

routine.when: everything else; run local Qwen first, escalate to escalation_model only on low-confidence or non-trivial diff
routine.tier5_note: When the high-RAM/GPU box arrives, repoint model (and optionally escalation_model) to a larger local model — e.g. qwen2.5-coder:72b / kimi / deepseek-class. A strong enough local model can also absorb the high tier, driving OpenAI review spend toward $0. Track under the Tier-5 local-model expansion plan.

third_voice.when: ROUTINE / non-sensitive diffs ONLY (ADR-029) — additive to the routine review, never auto-blocks. China-hosted: the helper hard-blocks (return 8) any high-stakes/sensitive content (auth/crypto/secret/payment/migration, sensitivity=high, domain-mode security|schema-migration) regardless of caller. Moved off the high tier — it must never see high-stakes code.

notes: ADR-025: adversarial review (reviewer, security-auditor, product-critic) is tiered by stakes via scripts/lib/review-router.sh — high → codex/gpt-5.5@high; routine → local Qwen first, escalate codex/gpt-5.4. Scope fixed to the diff (base..head), not whole-repo. Routes log to subagent-runs.jsonl (event:review_route) to verify the high/routine split. Cross-family (ADR-011) holds on both tiers.

v1.1.1 change: OpenAI/GPT-5.5-family work routes through the local Codex CLI (provider `codex`); Gemini-family work routes through the local Gemini CLI (provider `gemini`). Neither uses a direct API key. Adversarial roles run on a Claude model and orchestrate the respective CLI. See ADR-011 (Codex) and ADR-012 (Gemini).
v1.1 change: Foreman is a skill (skills/foreman/SKILL.md) in main-thread orchestration mode. Only in agent-teams mode is foreman a separate session (team-lead role).
Pricing verified against vendor docs on 2026-05-15. Anthropic: platform.claude.com. Apr 2026 friction was a 3.4x cost underforecast — verify live before any cost-sensitive operation.
/model-audit skill (Tier 4) re-verifies monthly and proposes changes.

Codex and Gemini calls are billed via their own CLI accounts and are NOT metered into cost_log — Tier 4 cost reporting does not see adversarial-review / red-team / architecture-critic / historian spend.
Gemini 3.1 Pro (2M context, released Feb 2026) — the Gemini CLI selects its own model; revisit the -m override in the next /model-audit.
